title: Stratified Countersubject
objective: Compose a countersubject in a different rhythmic stratum than the given subject (the Contrapunctus IX rule): double-speed cells riding above the subject, not a note-for-note harmonization.
prompt: Replace rests in V0 with a parser-valid counter-line against the given G-minor subject in V1: half-beat notes (duration 0.5) on every half-beat row from 0 through 7.5, and a final note of duration 2 at beat 8. The counter-line lives at DOUBLE the subject's prevailing rate - do not shadow the subject's attack pattern or its contour. Integer-beat tones must be consonant against the subject; dissonant half-beat tones must sit inside named cells (passing, neighbor, enclosure, escape). Shape the line as figurae cells with direction changes, one clear peak, and a leading-tone approach to the final octave or tenth.
success_criteria: Every row remains strict parser-valid MidGrid.
V1 subject remains unchanged, including hold cells.
V0 attacks on every half-beat row through 7.5 plus a final note at beat 8 - roughly double the subject's attack rate.
Integer-beat intervals are consonant; dissonant half-beat tones are cell interiors.
No rhythmic_homorhythm finding: the pair is rate-stratified, not harmonized.
Corrected solution has no evaluator errors.

interval_rules.label: Integer-beat cell boundaries must be consonant
